title: Make read-modify-write atomic
description: Use atomic commands, MULTI/EXEC with WATCH, or a Lua script so concurrent updates do not lose writes.
body: Use when updating a value based on its current contents (counter, balance, set membership). Do not GET then SET from app code, which loses updates under concurrency. Actions: prefer a single atomic command (INCRBY, HINCRBY, SETNX); for multi-key logic use a Lua script (runs atomically server-side) or MULTI/EXEC with WATCH for optimistic concurrency and retry on the watched-key change. Validation signals include a concurrency test where N parallel increments produce exactly N, not a lower number from lost updates. Risk boundary: a non-atomic read-modify-write on a counter that drives billing or limits silently undercounts and is a correctness finding.
